mecz: Muchová vs Nosková – Muchová to win
analiza: Muchova comes into this final with a very solid tournament profile, handling long rallies well and managing the tempo in big moments. Noskova has had a strong run, but over a longer match Muchova looks like the more stable player with a broader toolkit, so backing her to win makes sense at this price, even if it still sits in the risky bracket

Norway are a tricky opponent, but across the tournament England have shown more depth, a better balance between attack and defence, and stronger game management in knockout football. With the odds around 1.85–1.95 offering a bit of value over simple qualification, the most likely scripts are narrow England wins in 90 minutes, which makes England to win in regular time a reasonable, but still risky, angle rather than any kind of banker.

Spain have been the most reliable defensive side in the tournament, and that makes it hard to trust this game turning into another open Belgium shootout. Belgium’s recent scoring run looks a bit inflated by a wild game state against the USA, while Spain’s knockout profile points much more toward control than chaos.

This looks like a very even game, but it also has the ingredients for goals rather than a slow, cautious battle. Both sides have enough attacking quality to create chances, and once the match opens up, the Over 2.5 line becomes very playable.

Argentina look like the stronger and more complete side here, with the market, previews and team news all pointing in the same direction. Egypt still have enough quality to make the game awkward in spells, but Argentina carry more control, more depth and the cleaner path to winning this in normal time.

Argentina are still the stronger side on paper, but this does not look like a match that has to turn into an open, high-scoring game. Egypt should be more comfortable trying to keep things compact and survive in stages, which gives the under a better shape than chasing a bigger Argentina margin.
